case_metadata.case_name: Rivera Rodriguez v. Hospital San Cristobal
case_metadata.court: United States Court of Appeals For the First Circuit

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Rodríguez visited HSC complaining of pelvic pain. Rodríguez -- who was then seventy-one years old and living with several chronic health conditions including hypertension, type 2 diabetes, and asthma
evidence_index.summary: Rodríguez presented with pelvic pain and had chronic conditions.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Vélez performed Rodríguez's surgery on April 21, 2016, at HSC. During the surgery, Dr. Vélez discovered that Rodríguez had a 'frozen pelvis' with multiple 'intraabdominal adhesions,' and so she requested a surgical consultation from Dr. Mateo
evidence_index.summary: Surgery revealed a frozen pelvis; Dr. Mateo consulted.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: On April 25, 2016, Rodríguez was discharged from HSC after HSC staff observed 'positive bowel sounds' and Rodríguez reported 'positive stool passage.'
evidence_index.summary: Rodríguez discharged with positive bowel sounds and stool passage.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Four days later, on April 29, during a scheduled postoperative appointment with Dr. Vélez at HSC, Rodríguez reported that she had been experiencing 'nausea, vomiting, and abdominal/pelvic pain since April 26.'
evidence_index.summary: Post-op, Rodríguez reported nausea, vomiting, and pain.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Rodríguez was then admitted to HSC's emergency department and was diagnosed with a presumed perforated sigmoid colon.
evidence_index.summary: Admitted with presumed perforated sigmoid colon.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Rodríguez was subsequently admitted to HSC's intensive care unit ('ICU') in critical condition. She was intubated on a respirator with a nasogastric ('NG') tube, a colostomy bag, and a Foley catheter.
evidence_index.summary: Admitted to ICU in critical condition with multiple supports.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: On May 12, nursing and infectious-disease staff noted 'the presence of worms and/or maggots in the right nostril of [Rodríguez], where the NG tube was located.'
evidence_index.summary: Worms/maggots found in nostril where NG tube was placed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Rodríguez died later that evening, at 8:48 P.M. on May 19, 2016.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: An autopsy determined that Rodríguez's cause of death was 'peritonitis due to sigmoid colon perforation with associated sepsis and septic shock.'
evidence_index.summary: Autopsy: death due to peritonitis from colon perforation.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. James's expert report 'conclude[d] as a matter of fact that the defendants' negligence caused Rodríguez's premature death.'
evidence_index.summary: Dr. James concluded negligence caused premature death.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. James

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The judgment of the District Court is affirmed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

verdict_detected.key_phrase: The judgment of the District Court is affirmed.

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Rodríguez presented with pelvic pain and had chronic conditions including hypertension, type 2 diabetes, and asthma.

requestables.name: Surgical Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with Dr. Mateo during surgery

reveal.result_summary: Frozen pelvis with multiple intraabdominal adhesions

options.description: Admit to hospital for further evaluation
options.clinical_reasoning: Symptoms suggest possible complication requiring further evaluation
Inadequate response to potential serious complication

actual_action_defendant.description: Admitted to hospital for further evaluation

expected_action_court.description: Admit to hospital for further evaluation

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Not applicable as the correct action was taken

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ICU with critical condition
decision_points.prompt: What infection control measures should be implemented?

options.description: Regular inspection and cleaning of NG tube site

expected_action_court.description: Regular inspection and cleaning of NG tube site

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to prevent infection at NG tube site
